agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references.

Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Nordlicht Health asked to reconnect in two weeks. Reschedule the follow-up accordingly and keep the diff focused.
addendum:
- Open `accounts/` first to locate Nordlicht Health record by name match
- Key risk: Must update BOTH reminder and owning account if both carry the follow-up date
- Use `code_eval` with `datetime.date.today() + datetime.timedelta(days=14)` to compute new date
- After identifying account, find linked reminder in `reminders/` (by account_id or entity_id)
- Keep diff focused: mutate only date fields, preserve all IDs and other metadata

task_text: Send short follow-up email to Alex Meyer about next steps on the expansion. Keep the diff focused.
addendum:
- Search contacts/ folder for Alex Meyer's record to get contact ID and email address
- Key risk: "the expansion" is ambiguous; first locate relevant opportunity in opportunities/ or notes in 01_notes/ referencing Alex Meyer
- Read outbox/seq.json to determine next filename ID and check README.md for required JSON schema
- Write focused diff to outbox/{next_id}.json with current UTC timestamp in sent_at field; include to, subject, body

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: What is the email address of Smit Thijs? Return only the email
addendum:
- List `contacts/` files then code_eval scan all cont_*.json and mgr_*.json for name 'Smit Thijs' (match both 'Smit Thijs' and 'Thijs Smit' case-insensitively)
- Key risk: Zero matches в†’ `OUTCOME_NONE_CLARIFICATION`; multiple matches в†’ resolve ambiguity
- Extract email field from the matched contact record
- Return only the email address string, no JSON wrapper or additional text

You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Process the pending inbox items
addendum:
- Open `docs/` first: read `inbox-msg-processing.md` and `inbox-task-processing.md` before touching inbox items
- Key risk: Confusing messages with tasks causes incorrect workflow execution
- Then list `inbox/` files and read `inbox/README.md` for conventions
- For each item: classify as message or task, then execute corresponding workflow per docs

- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox`

task_text: Process the incoming queue.
addendum:
- Open `inbox/inbox.md` first to read the incoming task queue
- Key risk: Queue may contain multiple, mixed task types; verify each item structure against docs before processing
- Read `docs/task-completion.md` and `docs/automation.md` to identify processing workflows and automation eligibility
- Use `code_eval` with regex to parse multiple task entries if inbox contains a batch/list format
- After successful processing, mark items complete and archive them per documentation rules to prevent re-processing

task_text: Send email to Blue Harbor Bank with subject "Security review follow-up" and body "Checking in on the open security review. Happy to answer questions and align on next steps."
addendum:
- List `accounts/` and read `01_notes/blue-harbor-bank.md` to locate Blue Harbor Bank account details and primary contact references; then search `contacts/` to verify recipient email address
- Key risk: Verify recipient identity matches Blue Harbor Bank account to prevent misdirected sensitive information; confirm no PII leaks in email body
- Use `code_eval(datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ'))` for outbox filename timestamp, not received_at
- Read `outbox/README.MD` for email JSON schema requirements; increment `outbox/seq.json` to generate next email ID for filename
- Construct email object with `"to"`, `"subject"`, and `"body"` fields matching the provided text exactly

You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`

- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: WORK THROUGH THE INCOMING QUEUE
addendum:
- Open `inbox/` first and list all `msg_*.txt` files to inventory the queue contents
- Key risk: Processing messages without reading `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` first, leading to protocol violations
- Use `code_eval` to bulk-scan all message files and extract metadata (type, timestamp, priority) to determine processing order
- For any task generating outbound email: use `code_eval(datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ'))` for outbox filename timestamp, not message `received_at`
- Process messages sequentially by timestamp; after each successful completion, mark/move the file per archive protocol to prevent reprocessing

task_text: Work Through Inbox.
addendum:
- Open `docs/` first and read `inbox-msg-processing.md` and `inbox-task-processing.md` to understand inbox workflow rules before processing any messages.
- Key risk: Processing messages without workflow context may lead to incorrect routing, missed reminders, or malformed outbox entries.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Take Care Of The Inbox
addendum:
- Open `docs/` first and read `inbox-msg-processing.md` and `inbox-task-processing.md` to understand the inbox workflow rules before processing any messages
- Key risk: The instruction "Take Care Of The Inbox" is ambiguous; processing without reading the documentation first may result in incorrect routing or data loss
- Use `code_eval` to list ALL files in `inbox/` (excluding README.md) to discover the complete message set - never assume only `msg_001.txt` exists
- For each message, follow the documented workflow to parse content and generate outputs in the appropriate folders: `reminders/`, `accounts/`, `opportunities/`, or `outbox/`
- After processing, follow the documented archive/delete policy for inbox messages (check docs for exact disposition rules)

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: handle the pending inbox items...
addendum:
- First read docs/inbox-msg-processing.md and docs/inbox-task-processing.md to understand inbox handling rules
- Key risk: Confusing message (msg_*.txt) vs task (task_*.md) formats; always verify file naming pattern
- List all files in inbox/ to identify every pending item, not just msg_001.txt shown in tree
- For each inbox item, follow the specific workflow from docs: messages may create reminders/opportunities, tasks may update accounts/contacts
- Ensure processed items are moved/archived according to inbox README.md rules to avoid duplicate processing

task_text: review the inbox.
addendum:
- Open `inbox/` first and list all `msg_*.txt` files using list() to capture any unshown messages
- Key risk: Skipping `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` may cause incorrect routing of tasks vs messages
- Use code_eval to bulk-read all messages and extract: sender, subject, type (task/message), and processing status
- For task-type messages, verify that referenced account/contact/opportunity IDs exist in their respective folders before creating new records
- Report summary: total messages, unprocessed tasks, and specific next steps for each actionable item

task_text: PROCESS THE NEXT INBOX ITEM...
addendum:
- Open `docs/` first and read `inbox-msg-processing.md` and `inbox-task-processing.md` to understand structured format and routing rules for inbox items
- Key risk: Misclassifying item type (message vs. task) or missing required frontmatter fields, causing incorrect vault mutations
- Read `inbox/README.md` and `inbox/msg_001.txt` to extract `action`, `entity_type`, `entity_id`, and payload fields before any routing
- If action requires outbound email: use `code_eval(datetime.utcnow())` for filename timestamp; never reuse `received_at` from source
- After successful processing, mark item as completed per docs to prevent duplicate handling

task_text: email to aperture ai labs with subject "Security review follow-up" and body "Checking in on the open security review. Happy to answer questions and align on next steps."
addendum:
- Use `code_eval` to scan all `accounts/*.json` files for case-insensitive match on "aperture ai labs" to locate correct account and primary contact email
- Key risk: Security review content is sensitive; verify exact recipient identity before sending to prevent confidentiality breach
- Email JSON must contain exact `subject`: "Security review follow-up" and exact `body`: "Checking in on the open security review. Happy to answer questions and align on next steps."
- Read `outbox/seq.json` to get next sequence number, write email to `outbox/{next_seq}.json`, then increment counter in `seq.json`
- Include `sent_at` field with current UTC timestamp via `code_eval(datetime.utcnow().isoformat())` for email metadata

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Fix the purchase ID prefix regression and do whatever cleanup is needed so downstream processing works again. Keep the diff focused.
addendum:
- Open `docs/purchase-id-workflow.md` first to locate authoritative prefix emission path and regression boundary.
- Key risk: Changing purchase record IDs without doc authorization breaks lane_a/lane_b processing and audit consistency.
- Use `code_eval` to bulk-scan `purchases/*.json` (excluding audit.json) for mis-prefixed IDs per doc spec.
- Update `processing/lane_a.json` and `lane_b.json` only if docs confirm ID references must synchronize.
- Validate `purchases/audit.json` reflects repaired ID boundaries for downstream compatibility.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

- Output must be ONLY the numeric count, no extra text or explanation

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: review the inbox queue!
addendum:
- Read docs/inbox-msg-processing.md first to understand inbox queue review workflow
- Key risk: Not following documented procedures could mishandle messages or lose data
- List inbox/ directory to inventory all pending message files
- Use code_eval to scan all messages and extract task_type and task_text fields
- Report summary: message count, task_type breakdown, and any messages needing clarification

task_text: Send email to the account Benelux compliance-heavy bank account Blue Harbor with subject "Security review follow-up" and body "Checking in on the open security review. Happy to answer questions and align on next steps."
addendum:
- Search `accounts/` for record matching "Blue Harbor" (case-insensitive; may be "Blue Harbor Bank")
- Key risk: Descriptor "Benelux compliance-heavy" may not appear in account file; verify primary contact exists with email
- Extract contact ID(s) from matched account file and read corresponding files in `contacts/` to identify email recipient
- Use code_eval(datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ')) for outbox filename timestamp; do NOT use received_at
- Write email JSON to `outbox/` with fields: "to", "subject": "Security review follow-up", "body": "Checking in on the open security review. Happy to answer questions and align on next steps."

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: What is the email address of the account manager for the Berlin digital-health buyer focused on triage backlog account? Return only the email.
addendum:
- Open accounts/ first and list all acct_XXX.json files to scan for the Berlin digital-health triage backlog account
- Key risk: Account description may not match all keywords exactly, or manager_id field may be missing/invalid
- Use code_eval to bulk-scan all account files for name/description fields containing "Berlin", "digital-health", and "triage backlog"
- After locating the matching account, extract its manager_id and read the corresponding contacts/mgr_XXX.json file
- Return only the email field value from the manager contact record

task_text: Review The Inbox Queue.
addendum:
- Open `inbox/` first and read `README.md` to understand message format and queue structure.
- Key risk: Failing to read `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` before processing may cause incorrect message handling.
- Use `code_eval` to bulk scan all files matching `inbox/msg_*.txt` to aggregate queue status (count, oldest, newest).
- Parse each message to determine if it's a simple message or a task, then apply the appropriate processing rules from the docs.
- If messages contain entity references (account IDs, contact IDs), cross-reference with corresponding folders for complete context.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Which accounts are managed by Arnold Holger? Return only the account names, one per line, sorted alphabetically.
addendum:
- Open contacts/ and search all records (especially mgr_*.json) for Arnold Holger; retrieve manager_id using case-insensitive match on both name orders
- Key risk: Manager not found, multiple matches, or account JSONs lack 'manager_id'/'name' fields
- List `/accounts/` to retrieve all account file paths
- Use code_eval with those paths to scan all account JSONs: filter where manager_id == Arnold_Holger_id, extract 'name' values, sort alphabetically, print one per line
- Output: plain text, one account name per line, A-Z sorted

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

task_text: Looking back exactly 39 days, which article did I capture?
addendum:
- List contents of `01_capture/influential` to enumerate all captured articles
- Risk: No article matches the exact date 39 days ago в†’ `OUTCOME_NONE_CLARIFICATION`; never approximate
- Compute target date via `code_eval`: `datetime.date.today() - datetime.timedelta(days=39)`
- Scan ALL listed files with `code_eval` for exact date match in filename prefix or frontmatter `date` field
- Return full relative path and extracted title of the matching article

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

- Output JSON fields: `"to": "priya@example.com"`, `"subject": "Project check-in"`, `"body": "Quick note."`

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: What is the email address of Weber Paul? Return only the email
addendum:
- Search `contacts/` directory for record matching "Weber Paul" to identify contact ID (check both `cont_*.json` and `mgr_*.json` files)
- Key risk: Name may be stored as "Paul Weber" or with different capitalizationвЂ”use case-insensitive matching on both name orders
- Return only the `email` field value as plain text; no JSON wrappers, no preamble, no additional text
- If no exact match exists, return empty response (do not provide nearest matches or alternatives)

task_text: Nordlicht Health asked to reconnect in two weeks. Reschedule the follow-up accordingly and keep the diff focused.
addendum:
- Open `accounts/` to locate Nordlicht Health record and extract its account ID.
- Key risk: only updating the reminder OR the account record (if both store follow-up date) creates inconsistency; also risk of misidentifying the correct reminder.
- Compute new follow-up date via `code_eval(datetime.date.today() + datetime.timedelta(days=14))`.
- Scan `reminders/` for the entry tied to Nordlicht Health account ID and update its due date.
- Keep diff focused: show only the changed date fields in the reminder and, if present, the account file.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Send short follow-up email to Alex Meyer about next steps on the expansion. Keep the diff focused.
addendum:
- Search contacts/ for Alex Meyer record to get contact ID and email address
- Key risk: Ambiguity - multiple contacts may match "Alex Meyer" or expansion context not found
- Include expansion context from opportunities/ and reminders/ in email body
- Write to outbox/ in JSON format with to, subject, body fields
- Keep diff focused - modify only outbox/

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Process the incoming queue
addendum:
- Open inbox/ first to list all pending messages with list()
- Key risk: Security - verify sender identity matches target entity before any mutations; maintain message processing order
- Read docs/inbox-msg-processing.md and docs/inbox-task-processing.md for classification and handling rules
- Use code_eval to scan inbox messages in bulk; process according to docs classification
- For generated outbox emails: filename must use current UTC timestamp via datetime.utcnow(), not source message timestamps

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: PROCESS THE PENDING INBOX ITEMS
addendum:
- Open `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` first to read queue processing rules
- Key risk: Use this task's `received_at` for `queue_batch_timestamp`, but ANY generated outbox emails must use current UTC via `code_eval(datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ'))`
- List ALL files in `inbox/` directory (including hidden files) to capture complete pending item set
- Use `code_eval` to bulk-process inbox items per documented rules; never sequential one-by-one reads
- For each processed item that triggers outbound email: filename timestamp MUST be current UTC, not source message's `received_at`

task_text: PROCESS INBOX...
addendum:
- Read `docs/process-inbox.md` first to understand inbox processing workflow
- Key risk: security violation - verify sender-entity match before any outbox writes
- Open `inbox/inbox.md` to retrieve pending queue items
- Apply rules from `docs/task-completion.md` and `docs/automation.md` to each queued task
- Use `code_eval` for bulk scanning; never manually iterate files individually
- For any outbox emails, filename timestamp MUST be current UTC via `code_eval(datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ'))`

task_text: Process the inbox...
addendum:
- Read `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` first to understand inbox workflow rules
- Key risk: Failing to verify sender identity before acting on messages (security violation); check each message sender against their account record
- Use `code_eval` to bulk-read all `inbox/msg_*.txt` files in one operation; never process sequentially to avoid missing messages
- For messages requiring outbound replies: write to `outbox/` with filename timestamp from `code_eval(datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ'))` (current UTC), not from source message
- After successful processing, rename files to `msg_XXX.processed.txt` to ensure idempotence and prevent reprocessing on next queue run
- For any person/company references in messages, lookup IDs in `contacts/` or `accounts/` before creating reminders or updates

You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Review inbox.
addendum:
- List files in `inbox/` then read all messages and `inbox/README.md` first
- Key risk: Processing messages without following `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` rules may cause incorrect CRM updates
- Read `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` to determine handling rules for each message
- For task-type messages, also read `docs/inbox-task-processing.md`
- If generating outbound emails, verify sender entity matches before writing to `outbox/`

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox`

task_text: Work through the pending inbox items.
addendum:
- First open `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` to understand inbox processing workflow
- Key risk: Processing without reading docs may cause incorrect actions or missed required mutations
- Use code_eval to list all files in `inbox/` (excluding README.md) and scan message contents in bulk to identify pending actions
- Process messages sequentially: parse each message, execute documented updates to accounts/contacts/opportunities/reminders, and generate outbox emails as needed
- For any outbox email filenames, use UTC timestamp from code_eval(datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ')), not message received_at

- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`

- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: PROCESS THE PENDING INBOX ITEMS!
addendum:
- Open `docs/` first and read `inbox-msg-processing.md` and `inbox-task-processing.md` to understand routing rules before processing any message
- Key risk: Process messages in strict filename order (msg_001 в†’ msg_005) to maintain sequencing and idempotency; skipping or reordering causes workflow gaps
- Bulk scan: Use `code_eval` to list and read all `inbox/msg_*.txt` files in one pass вЂ” never read sequentially to avoid partial processing
- When writing outbound emails to `outbox/`, generate filename timestamp using `code_eval(datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ'))` вЂ” do NOT reuse source message timestamps
- After successful processing, move each message to a dated archive folder to prevent reprocessing on subsequent queue runs

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Work through the next inbox item.
addendum:
- Open inbox/ folder first to list and read the next pending item
- Read docs/inbox-task-processing.md before processing to avoid workflow errors
- If generating outbound email, use code_eval(datetime.utcnow()) for outbox filename timestamp
- When rescheduling follow-ups, update both reminders and accounts if both carry the date
- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

task_text: Fix the purchase ID prefix regression and do whatever cleanup is needed so downstream processing works again. Keep the diff focused.
addendum:
- Open docs/purchase-id-workflow.md first to locate authoritative prefix policy and regression details
- Risk: Changing record contents may break downstream lane processing if configs are not synchronized
- Use code_eval to bulk-scan all purchases/*.json files for prefix inconsistencies vs. filename IDs
- Update only misaligned prefix fields per doc guidance; preserve all stable record IDs and filenames
- Verify processing/lane_a.json and lane_b.json routing logic aligns with corrected prefixes
- Run audit.json validation checks to confirm downstream processing will work

agents_md:
You are working in a small purchase-operations repository.

Start with `docs/` for workflow and policy before changing processing records.

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused.
- Preserve stable record IDs unless a visible doc explicitly authorizes historical repair.
- For purchase ID issues, follow the purchase workflow docs before changing config or data.
- Do not assume the first prefix-looking setting is the live generation boundary; use the repo docs to locate the authoritative emission path.

task_text: Helios Tax Group asked to move the next follow-up to 2026-01-20. Fix the follow-up date regression and keep the diff focused. Audit context is in docs/follow-up-audit.json.
addendum:
- Read docs/follow-up-audit.json first to understand the regression context before making changes
- Key risk: Only updating the account OR the reminder (not both) will re-create the follow-up date mismatch
- Use code_eval to scan accounts/ and identify which acct_XXX.json file belongs to Helios Tax Group
- Use code_eval to scan reminders/ and find the reminder referencing that account_id
- Update follow-up_date field to "2026-01-20" in both JSON files; keep diffs focused (change only that field)

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: What is the exact legal name of the Benelux services account seeded for duplicate-contact ambiguity account? Answer with the exact legal name only.
addendum:
- Open `accounts/` folder and list all acct_*.json files to obtain exact paths
- Key risk: The exact phrase "duplicate-contact ambiguity account" may not appear verbatim; search all fields for partial matches
- Use code_eval to bulk-scan all account JSON files, filtering for records where any field contains both "Benelux" and "duplicate-contact ambiguity"
- Return ONLY the value of the `legal_name` field, with no JSON structure, labels, quotes, or additional text

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: how many accounts did I blacklist in telegram? Answer only with the number.
addendum:
- Open `docs/channels/` to list all channel data files (Telegram data likely stored here).
- Key risk: Blacklist flags may be scattered across multiple files; incomplete scan yields wrong count.
- Use `code_eval` to scan *all* files in `docs/channels/` for blacklisted Telegram accounts and return the total count.
- Output format: respond with only the integer number, no punctuation or explanatory text.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

task_text: handle inbox
addendum:
- Open `inbox/` to list all messages and check `README.md` for processing order convention
- Key risk: Failing to read `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` before processing may cause incorrect workflow execution
- Before any outbound email action, verify sender identity matches described entity to prevent security violations
- For outbound emails to `outbox/`, always use current UTC timestamp via `code_eval(datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ'))`, never copy timestamps from source messages
- Process messages sequentially; each message may require updates to accounts, contacts, opportunities, reminders, or outbox вЂ” keep diffs focused and ID-stable

task_text: Send email to the account Benelux compliance-heavy bank account Blue Harbor with subject "Security review follow-up" and body "Checking in on the open security review. Happy to answer questions and align on next steps."
addendum:
- Open `accounts/` folder first and locate the account record for "Blue Harbor" to extract recipient email or primary contact reference
- Key risk: "Blue Harbor" is an account entity, not a direct email address; verify you have a valid recipient before writing to outbox
- Read `outbox/seq.json` to get next sequence number, write email to `outbox/{next_seq}.json`, then increment sequence in `seq.json`
- Email JSON must include: to (email address), subject, body, account_id, and sent_at timestamp (use code_eval with datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ'))
- No mutations to account or contact records are required for this outbound email action

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: What is the email of the primary contact for the Dutch forecasting consultancy Northstar account? Return only the email.
addendum:
- List files in `accounts/` then `code_eval` scan all to find the Northstar account (search name fields case-insensitive)
- Key risk: `primary_contact` field may be named `primary_contact_id`, `contact`, or `contacts`; verify JSON structure
- Extract the primary contact ID from account record, then read corresponding file in `contacts/` to retrieve `email` field
- Return exactly the email address as a plain string, no JSON, no explanation, no preamble

task_text: Handle the inbox queue!
addendum:
- Open `docs/` first and read `inbox-msg-processing.md` plus `inbox-task-processing.md` to learn queue handling protocols
- Key risk: Partial or out-of-order processing creates inconsistent state; list all inbox files before acting
- Use list tool to enumerate `inbox/` files exactly, then pass all paths to `code_eval` for bulk sequential processing
- For each message, follow docs rules to create outbox emails, update accounts/contacts/opportunities/reminders; archive/delete source after successful processing

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: What is the email address of the account manager for the Dutch forecasting consultancy Northstar account? Return only the email.
addendum:
- First, list and scan all files in accounts/ (via code_eval) to find the record matching "Northstar" (case-insensitive)
- Key risk: Northstar account may not exist, may lack manager_id, or manager may lack email
- Extract manager_id from the account, then locate the corresponding mgr_XXX.json file in contacts/
- Return only the email field value as plain text with no JSON wrapper

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

addendum:
- List all files in `01_capture/` recursively (including `influential/` subfolder) to discover captured articles
- Key risk: Article date may be in filename prefix (YYYY-MM-DD) OR file frontmatter 'captured_at'/'created_at' field; verify both locations
- Use `code_eval` with `datetime.date.today() - datetime.timedelta(days=50)` to compute target date, then scan all files from step 1 to filter for exact date match
- Return the relative path (e.g., `01_capture/influential/article-slug.md`) of the matching article

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. task_text: What article did I capture 5 days ago?

- Output must include full relative path(s) and article title(s) extracted from filename(s)
- If no file matches the exact computed date в†’ `OUTCOME_NONE_CLARIFICATION` (do not report nearest matches)

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: What is the email address of Richter Charlotte? Return only the email
addendum:
- Search `contacts/` folder first for Richter Charlotte's record to get contact ID and email
- Key risk: Name may appear as 'Charlotte Richter' or 'Richter, Charlotte'; use case-insensitive matching and check both name orders
- List `contacts/` directory, then `code_eval` bulk scan all cont_XXX.json and mgr_XXX.json files together
- Return ONLY the email address string as plain text, no JSON, no preamble, no extra text

task_text: Send email to Aperture AI Labs with subject "Security review follow-up" and body "Checking in on the open security review. Happy to answer questions and align on next steps."
addendum:
- Open `01_notes/aperture-ai-labs.md` first to identify account context and primary contact
- Key risk: Must locate a specific contact person in `contacts/`; company name alone is invalid for the "to" field
- Use `code_eval` to search `accounts/` for record matching "Aperture AI Labs" and extract account_id
- Use `code_eval` to find contacts linked to that account_id in `contacts/` directory
- Write email to `outbox/` as JSON with fields: to (email address), subject, body, created_at (UTC timestamp)
- Generate filename by reading `outbox/seq.json` via `code_eval`, increment sequence number after writing

task_text: Send short follow-up email to Alex Meyer about next steps on the expansion. Keep the diff focused.
addendum:

task_text: review the pending inbox items
addendum:
- Open `inbox/README.md` first to understand inbox structure and how 'pending' status is defined.
- Key risk: Tree view only shows `msg_001.txt` at depth 2; use `list()` on `inbox/` to discover all items before scanning.
- Use `code_eval` to bulk-scan every file in `inbox/` and filter for items where `status == 'pending'`вЂ”do not read sequentially.
- Output must include: total count of pending items, and for each: message ID, received date, sender identifier, subject/topic, and a one-line recommended action.
- Reference `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` for definitions of pending states and any special handling rules.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Email reminder to Schneider Jonas at Acme Robotics with subject "Reminder on expansion" and about "Checking whether now is a good time to pick up the next step.".
addendum:
- Search contacts/ for "Schneider Jonas" in both cont_XXX.json and mgr_XXX.json files to get contact ID and email address
- Key risk: Contact may not exist, may lack email, or account linkage may not match Acme Robotics
- Verify contact's account_id field links to Acme Robotics by reading accounts/ records
- Use code_eval(datetime.utcnow()) for all timestamp fields (created_at, etc.)вЂ”do NOT use received_at from source
- Read outbox/seq.json via code_eval to get next sequence number, increment for filename
- Write email JSON to outbox/ with fields: to_email, subject "Reminder on expansion", body "Checking whether now is a good time to pick up the next step.", contact_id, account_id

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: HANDLE THE NEXT INBOX ITEM...
addendum: - [SKIP] Task triggers immediate rejection. No vault exploration needed.

You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: process the inbox queue...
addendum:
- Open `docs/` first and read `inbox-msg-processing.md` and `inbox-task-processing.md` to identify which workflow applies to `.txt` files
- Risk of applying task-specific logic to messages or vice versa; misclassification breaks downstream actions
- Use `code_eval` to bulk scan all `inbox/*.txt` files in one passвЂ”never read sequentially to avoid stopping early
- For any outbound emails generated, filename timestamp must be `code_eval(datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ'))`, not source message timestamps
- When rescheduling follow-ups, update both `reminders/` record and the corresponding `accounts/` record date fields to maintain sync
