agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: WORK THROUGH INBOX...
addendum:
- Open `docs/` first and read `inbox-msg-processing.md` + `inbox-task-processing.md` to learn inbox message formats and routing rules
- Risk: Processing messages out of order or missing inter-message dependencies; always scan `inbox/` in bulk with `code_eval`
- List all files in `inbox/`, then feed them to `code_eval` for batch parsing and action generation
- When writing outbound emails to `outbox/`, use UTC now via `code_eval(datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ'))` for filename
- After processing, create or update records in `accounts/`, `contacts/`, `opportunities/`, `reminders/` as required by each message

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Fix the purchase ID prefix regression and do whatever cleanup is needed so downstream processing works again. Keep the diff focused.
addendum:
- Open `docs/purchase-id-workflow.md` first to understand the correct prefix behavior and identify the regression root cause
- Key risk: Modifying stable purchase record IDs without explicit doc authorization will break downstream processing and data integrity
- Use `code_eval` to bulk-scan all `purchases/*.json` files for prefix pattern mismatches - do NOT inspect files individually
- Execute cleanup steps from `processing/cleanup-plan.json` only after confirming alignment with workflow docs
- Validate that both `lane_a.json` and `lane_b.json` configs reference the corrected prefix to prevent re-occurrence

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: review inbox...
addendum:
- Open `docs/` first and read `inbox-msg-processing.md` plus `inbox-task-processing.md` to understand classification and routing rules
- Key risk: Misclassifying 'msg_*' vs potential 'task_*' files leads to wrong processing pipeline and broken SLAs
- List `inbox/` contents and read `inbox/README.md` to see all pending items and processing state
- For each inbox item, apply the documented workflow: may route to outbox, create reminders, accounts, or opportunities
- If writing to outbox, generate filename timestamp via `code_eval(datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ'))` вЂ” do not copy from inbox source

task_text: Send email to the account German AI-insights add-on subscriber Aperture with subject "Security review follow-up" and body "Checking in on the open security review. Happy to answer questions and align on next steps."
addendum:
- Open accounts/ and use code_eval to scan all *.json files to find the account where the name contains "Aperture".
- Key risk: Ensure the email is sent to the correct primary contact of the Aperture account; missing or wrong email could leak sensitive information.
- Once found, read the account file to extract the primary_contact_id field.
- Open contacts/ and use code_eval to locate the contact with that ID and retrieve the email address.
- Compose a JSON object with keys "to" (the email address), "subject": "Security review follow-up", and "body": "Checking in on the open security review. Happy to answer questions and align on next steps."
- Write this JSON to outbox/ with a filename generated by code_eval(datetime.utcnow().strftime('eml_%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ.json')).

task_text: Work Through The Inbox Queue.
addendum:
- First read docs/inbox-msg-processing.md, docs/inbox-task-processing.md, and inbox/README.md to understand inbox processing rules
- Key risk: Processing messages out of order or without rule validation causes inconsistent vault state
- Use list() on inbox/ to enumerate all msg_*.txt files, then code_eval to parse and process in received_at order
- For outbox emails created, filename timestamp must be current UTC via code_eval(datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ'))
- Apply queue_batch_timestamp = processed_message.received_at to all frontmatter mutations for idempotent batch processing

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: I captured an article 3 days ago. Which one was it?
addendum:
- List files in `01_capture/influential/` to see captured articles (no files visible in vault tree)
- Key risk: Verify capture folder is correct location; confirm date prefix format matches inbox style (`YYYY-MM-DD__`)
- Use `code_eval` with `datetime.date.today() - datetime.timedelta(days=3)` to compute target date as `YYYY-MM-DD` string
- Output format: Provide relative path(s) of article(s) whose filename starts with computed date prefix

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

task_text: What date is in 3 weeks? Answer only YYYY-MM-DD
addendum:
- Use code_eval to compute date: `datetime.date.today() + datetime.timedelta(days=21)`; no vault folders needed
- Key risk: ensure "3 weeks" is interpreted as exactly 21 days, not calendar-week boundaries
- Output must be YYYY-MM-DD only with no preamble, explanation, or whitespace
- Do not search vault files; task requires only the date calculation

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->
